title: 株式会社アルバック 有価証券報告書（第121期，2024年7月～2025年6月） (ULVAC, Inc. Securities Report, FY ended June 2025, 121st fiscal period)
authors_or_org: ULVAC, Inc. (株式会社アルバック)
claim_supported: ULVAC's mandatory Japanese securities report (有価証券報告書) for FY ended June 2025 discloses vacuum-application-business orders received of ¥521.37 billion, sales of ¥521.34 billion, and order backlog of ¥174.1 billion — ULVAC being a top-tier global vacuum/thin-film equipment supplier to semiconductor and display fabs.
locator: official EDINET-linked securities report PDF (search-index-derived figures; full document not line-item-fetched this session)
notes: Equivalent in legal status to a US 10-K; ULVAC is one of the six vendors said to hold ~62% of global semiconductor vacuum-pump revenue (see L07-039).

title: INFICON Holding AG — Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Results
authors_or_org: INFICON Holding AG
claim_supported: INFICON (Swiss-listed) reported FY2025 full-year sales of $673.7M (+0.4% YoY); its largest segment, Semi & Vacuum Coating (leak detection, gas analysis, vacuum gauges), is roughly half of total revenue; Asia-Pacific sales rose 19.2% to ~$169M on AI-driven semiconductor demand and China re-acceleration, while Americas sales fell 12.4% to ~$156M on US trade-restriction impacts.
locator: earnings announcement/results summary (search index only; full annual report and earnings call transcript not independently fetched this session)
notes: Named incumbent leak-detection/vacuum-instrumentation vendor; the US-vs-Asia revenue divergence is a direct, dated (FY2025) data point on export-control impact in this lane.

title: SBIR Award: Densified Cryogen System for Maximum Storage and Delivery Performance in Zero-Gravity (Sierra Lobo, Inc.)
authors_or_org: US Department of Defense, Missile Defense Agency (MDA); recipient Sierra Lobo, Inc.
claim_supported: A DOD/Missile Defense Agency SBIR Phase II award ($495,907) funded Sierra Lobo, Inc. to develop a densified-cryogen storage system with a lightweight sensor array for cryogenic temperature and vapor/liquid (leak-relevant) detection in zero-gravity.
locator: sbir.gov award record page, fetched directly
notes: Older award (2003) surfaced by a query aimed at recent DOE grants; included with corrected agency/date/recipient rather than discarded, since it is a directly-fetched, on-topic government award record for cryogenic leak/vapor sensing. Illustrates that government funding for this niche is intermittent rather than a steady recent pipeline — flagged as an uncertainty in the lane brief.
